libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: No environment variable or system property setup is required. If generated tests load config files, use core-supported Java .properties/.props or in-memory MapPropertySource; YAML/JSON/TOML/HOCON examples would require separate Hoplite format modules and should not be assumed for hoplite-core-only coverage.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8367 [Automation] native-image run fails for com.sksamuel.hoplite:hoplite-core:3.0.0.RC1; label=fails-native-image-run; body_chars=8015

risks: Documentation says non-properties formats require separate Hoplite modules, so tests that choose YAML, JSON, TOML, or HOCON without adding a format dependency will fail for reasons unrelated to hoplite-core metadata.
Version 3.0.0.RC1 updates Kotlin runtime dependencies to Kotlin 2.1.0, but those are normal transitive dependencies rather than deterministic setup.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: hoplite-core is usable with its normal transitive dependencies and built-in core APIs; meaningful coverage is reachable via MapPropertySource or built-in Java properties parsing without Docker, extra Maven modules, or required environment/system properties.
